project.name: Decentralized Identity Architecture for Interoperable E-Government Sentinels
project.primary_goal: Evolve an existing Sentinel-based e-government interoperability model by preserving centralized service discovery while decentralizing service-to-service authentication and authorization.

current_state.summary: The current interoperability model uses Sentinel components as producers and consumers, with a central discovery/routing point. Authentication and authorization are effectively centralized.
observed_limitations: Limited autonomy for independently operated government entities
Weak architectural separation between discovery and trust

target_state.summary: Keep service discovery centralized, but move authentication and authorization to a decentralized trust model based on DID/VC/VP and possibly smart contracts.
desired_properties: Mutual authentication between Sentinels from different government entities
Revocation and lifecycle management without a single central authority
Minimal disruption to existing communication flow
Compatibility with HTTP as baseline, with extensibility to DIDComm and MQTT

government_entities.description: Independent government organizations, each operating and maintaining its own Sentinel instance(s).
assumptions: Each government entity installs its own Sentinel
Each government entity is responsible for updating and operating its own Sentinel
Administrative isolation between entities is assumed

sentinels.description: Gateway/agent components that mediate inter-entity requests and implement decentralized identity, credential handling, policy evaluation, and revocation checks.

central_discovery_router.description: Centralized service catalog and message router/forwarder.
central_discovery_router.important_note: It is not an authorization authority in the proposed architecture.

distributed_trust_layer.description: Ledger-based trust/governance layer storing stable identity/trust metadata, not volatile operational endpoint data.

in_scope: Protocol strategy for HTTP, DIDComm, and MQTT
out_of_scope: Detailed UI or service business logic

identity_scope.subject_type: services and service gateways, not end-users
identity_scope.explicit_note: This project focuses on decentralized identity for machine-to-machine / service-to-service communication.

ledger_preference.note: Ethereum is preferred by the project sponsor, but alternatives are acceptable if architecturally better.

pki.decision: Existing government PKI is not mandatory, but must be researched and accommodated as a compatibility/adaptation path.

transports.decision: Retain HTTP-based messages as the primary operational baseline while designing a transport-agnostic trust layer.

high_level_architecture.summary: A hybrid architecture with centralized discovery and decentralized trust.

layers.name: Operational Layer
layers.responsibility: Service invocation, routing, local proxying, operational endpoint management

layers.name: Trust & Governance Layer
layers.responsibility: Identity anchoring, issuer trust, credential status, governance metadata

high_level_architecture.design_principle: Keep dynamic operational data off-chain and stable trust metadata on-chain.

key_architectural_decisions.reason: Preserves existing communication flow and operational simplicity.

key_architectural_decisions.decision: Decentralize authentication and authorization using DID, VC, VP, and optionally smart-contract-backed trust metadata.
key_architectural_decisions.reason: Removes dependence on a single central trust authority.
Minimizes required changes to existing internal services and makes the retrofit pattern practical.

key_architectural_decisions.decision: Use environment-aware authorization as a first-class requirement.
key_architectural_decisions.reason: Strong dev/test/prod separation is a core project requirement and a likely novelty area.

key_architectural_decisions.decision: Do not store live service availability endpoints directly in smart contracts.
key_architectural_decisions.reason: Endpoints are operationally dynamic and unsuitable as primary on-chain data.

key_architectural_decisions.decision: Store trust metadata on-chain and operational endpoint metadata in central discovery.
key_architectural_decisions.reason: Clean separation between stable trust anchors and dynamic service-discovery state.

key_architectural_decisions.decision: Producer Sentinels may sign service descriptors distributed by central discovery.
key_architectural_decisions.reason: Enables cryptographic integrity for service metadata without putting volatile endpoint state on-chain.

key_architectural_decisions.decision: Use bounded revocation freshness guarantees instead of assuming perfectly fresh global state.
key_architectural_decisions.reason: This is realistic for outages and provides a strong, formalizable security property.

identity_subjects.subject: Sentinel
identity_subjects.why: The Sentinel is the externally visible gateway and trust enforcement point for a government entity.

optional_extension.note: Per-service DIDs were considered possible, but the working model uses Sentinel-level DIDs for simplicity and retrofit friendliness.

environment_model.preferred_approach: Separate DIDs per environment, optionally combined with audience/channel binding in the VP.
alternatives_considered: Single DID with env claim only
environment_model.current_preference_reason: Separate DIDs per environment provide clearer non-confusion guarantees and simpler reasoning.

artifacts.did: Identifier for a Sentinel or other service principal
artifacts.vc: Credential expressing access rights or trust assertions
artifacts.vp: Runtime presentation proving possession of valid credentials and DID key control

credential_model.likely_issuer: Typically the Producer-side authority / service owner / government entity exposing the service
credential_purpose: Authorize one Sentinel to call services exposed by another
Bind rights to a specific environment and optionally to a specific audience/provider
example_claims: scope = allowed service(s), endpoint(s), method(s), attributes
statusRef = reference to credential status mechanism

authorization_model.objective: Define a formal authorization model suitable for academic contribution.
authorization_model.decision_function: Decide(B, A, r, e, ctx) -> Permit | Deny

definitions.ctx: Runtime context including time, nonce, transport/channel binding, revocation state, etc.

required_conditions_for_permit: A proves control of its DID key
A presents VC(s) satisfying policy for resource r and environment e
Credential status is acceptable under freshness policy
The VP is audience-bound and environment-bound
Any channel-binding / anti-replay requirements are satisfied

policy_style.note: This remains flexible; the architecture supports either as long as the decision logic is formalized.

environment_binding.threat_addressed: Cross-environment replay or confused-deputy failures between dev/test/prod

chosen_direction.secondary: Bind VP proof to audience, environment, nonce, and channel binding data

environment_binding.security_goal: A credential or presentation intended for one environment should not be reusable in another environment.

revocation_model.importance: Core functionality and likely research contribution area
revocation_model.intended_guarantee: If a credential is revoked at time t_r, then any request accepted after t_r + Δ must not be authorized by that credential.

sentinel_behavior.normal_mode: Use cached revocation view if it is fresh enough
sentinel_behavior.note: Evaluation should compare these strategies under outage scenarios.

accepted_uses: Optional hash anchoring of signed service descriptors
rejected_primary_use: Acting as the primary dynamic service discovery store
smart_contract_role.reason_for_rejection: On-chain state is too rigid, costly, and leaky for volatile operational endpoint data.

central_discovery_non_role: Not the place where credential trust is decided
service_discovery_model.verification_expectation: Discovery-provided service metadata may be signed by the Producer Sentinel and validated against on-chain trust state.

service_descriptor_pattern.description: Producer Sentinel signs a service descriptor containing current operational endpoint metadata, and central discovery stores/distributes it.
verification_steps: Verify the descriptor signature against the Producer Sentinel DID
Verify that the Producer Sentinel DID is authorized for that service/environment according to on-chain trust metadata
Use descriptor as current operational resolution material
service_descriptor_pattern.optional_enhancement: Anchor hash(serviceDescriptor) on-chain for tamper evidence and auditing.

communication_model.architectural_rule: Authorization semantics should remain transport-agnostic; only the transport bindings differ.

anti_replay_and_binding.http: Bind VP to TLS exporter and/or request-specific nonce + audience + env
anti_replay_and_binding.mqtt: Bind VP to MQTT session/channel/topic context

steps: Producer Sentinel prepares service metadata
Producer Sentinel may sign a service descriptor
Producer Sentinel registers service metadata in Central Discovery
Optional: trust anchor or descriptor hash is anchored on-chain
Producer-side authority decides to grant access to Consumer Sentinel
Issuer creates VC for Consumer Sentinel DID
VC includes audience/environment/scope/expiry/status reference
Consumer Sentinel stores VC in its credential wallet

flow_3_request_execution.purpose: Runtime call from consumer-side service to producer-side service
steps: Consumer Service calls its local Consumer Sentinel
Consumer Sentinel resolves route via Central Discovery if needed
Consumer Sentinel creates a VP using relevant VC(s)
VP proof is bound to aud=DID(Producer Sentinel), env, nonce, and transport-specific binding
Consumer Sentinel sends request via HTTP/DIDComm/MQTT, typically through Central Discovery
Central Discovery forwards/reroutes but does not make final auth decisions
Producer Sentinel receives request
Producer Sentinel verifies DID ownership proof, VC signature, issuer trust, environment match, and revocation freshness
Producer Sentinel evaluates Decide(B,A,r,e,ctx)
If Permit, request is forwarded internally to Producer Service
If Deny, request is rejected

flow_4_revocation.purpose: Invalidate access after compromise or policy change
steps: Authorized revoker updates credential status in revocation/status mechanism
Producer Sentinels refresh status according to strategy
After freshness bound Δ, revoked credentials must no longer authorize requests

flow_5_key_rotation_or_did_update.purpose: Recover from key compromise or perform maintenance
steps: Sentinel controller updates DID registry with new key material or deactivates old DID
Other Sentinels resolve updated DID state
Subsequent VP verification uses new trust material

sentinel_internal_components.description: Each Sentinel is conceived as a DID-enabled gateway/agent with the following internal modules.

modules.name: DID Key Store
modules.responsibility: Holds Sentinel DID keys, ideally environment-specific

modules.name: VP Builder / Verifier
modules.responsibility: Constructs presentations and verifies received proofs/credentials

modules.name: Revocation Manager
modules.responsibility: Tracks status freshness, cache, Delta-based revocation behavior

modules.name: Transport Adapter
modules.responsibility: Integrates the common trust model with HTTP, DIDComm, MQTT

modules.name: Service Descriptor Signer
modules.responsibility: Signs service metadata distributed through discovery

did_registry.purpose: Map DIDs to public keys, service metadata references, rotation/deactivation state

trust_registry.purpose: Represent trusted issuers and/or authorized credential types/schemas

rejected_designs.name: Store live endpoint directly in smart contract
rejected_designs.reason: Endpoint data is dynamic operational state, not stable trust metadata.

rejected_designs.name: Use smart contract as primary health/availability registry
rejected_designs.reason: Health is highly dynamic and unsuitable for on-chain state.

rejected_designs.name: Make central discovery the runtime authorization authority
rejected_designs.reason: The architecture aims to separate routing/discovery from decentralized trust enforcement.

research_direction.main_research_plan: Define a formal authorization model + environment binding + revocation freshness guarantees, then implement it in a Sentinel testbed and evaluate compromise scenarios + outage scenarios.
research_direction.why_this_path: This is the most promising route to achieve moderate-to-high academic novelty.
novelty_target: Formal authorization semantics for VC-based service access
Hybrid centralized discovery + decentralized trust security model

testbed.goal: Implement a realistic Sentinel-based prototype across multiple entities/environments/transports.

compromise_scenarios.name: Credential theft without private key theft
compromise_scenarios.expected_outcome: Attack should fail because attacker cannot create valid holder proof.

compromise_scenarios.name: Consumer Sentinel DID key compromise
compromise_scenarios.expected_outcome: Attack initially succeeds; containment depends on revocation/key rotation and freshness bound Δ.

compromise_scenarios.name: Issuer compromise
compromise_scenarios.expected_outcome: System must rely on trust registry governance and issuer deauthorization.

compromise_scenarios.name: Central discovery compromise
compromise_scenarios.expected_outcome: Routing may be disrupted, but unauthorized access should still fail if audience/env/channel binding is correct.

outage_scenarios.name: Ledger unreachable
questions: Can cached DID and status data be used safely?
What policy applies after freshness window expires?

outage_scenarios.name: Revocation feed/status update outage
questions: How do availability and security trade off under stale status?

outage_scenarios.name: Clock skew/time sync problems
questions: How much skew can the system tolerate before freshness and expiry logic becomes unreliable?

key_metrics: Attack success probability under each threat scenario
Operational overhead (cache size, update rate, trust anchor complexity)

not_claimed_as_novel: Using blockchain as a trust anchor in general
claimed_as_potentially_novel: A formalized Sentinel-based decentralized authorization model for inter-government machine-to-machine interoperability
Environment-scoped service credentials with strong non-confusion guarantees
Bounded revocation freshness guarantees in a Sentinel architecture
A rigorous hybrid model where discovery remains centralized while trust is decentralized
academic_positioning.paper_type: Reference architecture + formal model + prototype evaluation

intended_main_static_diagram_elements: VC issuance and runtime verification notes

core_security_properties_desired: Separation of operational discovery from trust authority

important_do_not_change_without_explicit: Do not move live endpoints onto the smart contract as primary source of truth
Do not turn Central Discovery into the authorization authority
Do not collapse environment separation into a weak string-only claim without cryptographic/environment-specific bindings
Do not make the transport-specific bindings define the authorization semantics; keep semantics transport-agnostic
areas_still_open_for refinement: Exact VC encoding format (JWT VC vs JSON-LD VC, etc.)
Whether trust registry is on-chain, off-chain, or hybrid
Whether environment separation uses only separate DIDs or also per-env derived keys
Choice of Ethereum mainnet vs permissioned Ethereum vs alternative ledger
preferred_next_steps: Define the VP binding schema for HTTP/DIDComm/MQTT
Specify Delta-based revocation policy behavior under outages
Build a Sentinel testbed architecture document
Create sequence diagrams for runtime request and revocation flows

one_line_summary: Build a Sentinel-based, government-to-government interoperability architecture in which centralized discovery is preserved, but service identity, authorization, and revocation are decentralized via DID/VC/VP and a ledger-backed trust layer, with strong environment separation and bounded revocation freshness guarantees.
